destinations.evidence: The cache stores Option<ModelInfo>, so both found models and not-found None results are cached without changing the registry lookup result.
LLM pricing resolves the exact decoded model name through the cached registry reader and still errors when the model is absent; executor metadata is explicitly ignored during response decoding.
The public fee calculation path threads ModelCache into LLM pricing and rejects unsupported fee paths rather than falling back to unrelated metadata.

equivalence_argument: The cache preserves exact-match registry semantics because it caches the exact Option<ModelInfo> result for the exact model_name key. resolve_llm_model_config converts cached None into the same missing-model error and has no fallback to normalized names, default pricing, or executor-supplied metadata.
notes: This is the same cache path as invariant 415, with the missing-model branch as the important behavior.
rationale: The cache deliberately stores Option<ModelInfo>, including None for not-found models, and the LLM resolver still states that missing models cause the transaction to be skipped with no fallback. Caching must not weaken the consensus-sensitive exact model lookup rule. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: The ModelCache must preserve exact-match ModelRegistry semantics: a model name not found in storage is cached as None and subsequent fee calculations for that same model in the same block must return the same missing-model error rather than falling back to executor metadata, normalized names, or default pricing.
suggested_falsification_tests: Run two LLM fee calculations in the same block for an absent model name. Assert the first lookup returns a missing-model error and populates the cache with None, the second returns the same error without a storage reread, and neither produces a fallback executor fee.
summary: Missing LLM models remain cached exact-match failures.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Run two LLM fee calculations in the same block for an absent model name. Assert the first lookup returns a missing-model error and populates the cache with None, the second returns the same error without a storage reread, and neither produces a fallback executor fee.
title: Missing LLM models remain exact-match failures
